title: External test build smoke
summary: Smoke Web and Windows external-test build artifacts across launch, load, save/export/import, input and diagnostics after prerequisite M6 product-gate slices land.
acceptance: Windows external-test build starts from a clean artifact path
Web build starts from a product-gate target
Smoke covers launch, load, save/export/import where available, basic input and diagnostics
Smoke exercises M5 product-gate surfaces and not only an empty shell

history.details: Instantiated from reviewed WM-0084 M6 DAG; keep proposed until all prerequisite product-gate slices are done.

objective: Consolidate Web and Windows external-test smoke evidence after platform, storage, security, accessibility and diagnostics gates.
risksAndFileOwnership: Risk: build smoke passes without meaningful scenario coverage.
Risk: external-test smoke grows into release/store work.
File ownership is limited to smoke tooling, docs and reports.
ownerGate: Block if smoke does not exercise product gate surfaces
Block on public release upload, signing credentials or telemetry
Block if smoke hides prerequisite gate failures
m0M5RegressionCoverage: Smoke does not alter simulation baselines
M5 product-gate surfaces must be visible in the smoke path
benchmarkImpact: May record external smoke timing and artifact paths; no benchmark threshold changes.
reviewRouting: qa-performance completes to reviewer; reviewer verifies smoke coverage and non-release boundaries.
rollbackModel: Revert smoke tooling/docs changes and leave product decision blocked on external-test smoke evidence.

sparkEligibility.reason: External-test smoke spans product build validation and is not a small Spark slice.
